title: Multi-environment outage — domain ownership conflict
rootCause: AWS Amplify domain ownership conflict combined with region discrepancy and missing IAM service role.
summary: During deployment of a new monitoring pipeline project, configuring the root domain on a second Amplify app transferred internal CloudFront ownership away from the main site, taking down all environments. The original app was located in a different AWS region than where recovery was being attempted, extending the investigation window. A missing IAM service role was a secondary blocker. Resolved by identifying the original app in us-east-1, recreating the IAM role, and restoring domain configuration.
lessonsLearned: Only one Amplify app can own a root domain — all subdomains must be configured under the same app
Always verify AWS console region before creating or modifying resources
IAM service roles prefixed with AWS are infrastructure-critical and should not be removed
Remove domain configuration before deleting an Amplify app to prevent orphaned CloudFront associations
Validate new domain configurations on isolated subdomains before touching a shared root domain

title: api.aldenbryce.com — DNS resolution failure due to malformed CNAME value
rootCause: Route 53 CNAME record value contained an https:// protocol prefix, which is invalid for DNS records. Initial DNS caching masked the misconfiguration until all caches expired overnight.
summary: api.aldenbryce.com was initially working after deployment on March 23 due to DNS caching, but became unreachable overnight once all caches expired. The CNAME record was created with https://energy-api-yne8.onrender.com instead of the bare hostname. DNS resolvers returned NXDOMAIN on fresh lookups. The Render service was healthy throughout. Resolved by removing the https:// prefix from the Route 53 CNAME value.
lessonsLearned: CNAME values must be bare hostnames - no protocol prefix, no path, no port
DNS caching can mask misconfiguration - always verify with a fresh incognito window or flushed DNS cache after setup
When a domain is unreachable, test the underlying service URL directly first to isolate DNS vs application failure
Managed platform domain verification does not validate upstream DNS record format - verify end-to-end from a browser
